name: 5 Star Car Title Loans
description_short: 5 Star Car Title Loans offers secured auto title loans up to $50,000 with same-day funding, allowing borrowers to keep their vehicle while using its equity as collateral.
description_long:
5 Star Car Title Loans has been operating since 2012 and claims to have funded over 255,000 loans across nearly 18 states. The company is headquartered in San Bernardino, California, and operates physical locations in states including California, Florida, Indiana, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Ohio, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, and Washington. They position themselves as a licensed and regulated lender operating in the title loan space.

5 Star Car Title Loans specializes in auto title loans where borrowers use their vehicle's title as collateral to secure cash amounts ranging from $100 to $50,000. Beyond traditional car title loans, they also offer motorcycle title loans, semi-truck title loans, installment loans with terms up to 24 months, and same-day loans. The company emphasizes that applicants can keep their vehicle while borrowing against its equity and advertises flexible repayment terms with no prepayment penalties. They market their service to borrowers with imperfect credit, stating that applicants may still qualify despite credit challenges.

The company distinguishes itself through claimed transparency ("clear, upfront terms with no hidden fees"), same-day funding capability for qualified applicants, online and in-person application options, and a quick approval process (decision within minutes according to their materials). They highlight 256-bit SSL and TLS security for online applications and maintain a 4.7-star rating based on 11 customer reviews on their website. Customer testimonials emphasize speed, helpful staff, and hassle-free processes.

As a title loan lender, 5 Star Car Title Loans operates in a high-cost borrowing category where APR and finance charges are typically substantial and vary significantly by state regulation. While they advertise no prepayment penalties and flexible terms, title loans inherently carry risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed. The lack of specific APR disclosure on their website and variation by state means borrowers must obtain quotes to understand true costs. Their service is appropriate for genuine emergency situations but should be considered a last resort given the collateral risk and likely high cost of borrowing.
address: 2409 Arbor Pointe Dr Apt 3, Louisville, KY 40220

company_info.headquarters: 2409 Arbor Pointe Dr Apt 3, Louisville, KY 40220

pros: Loan amounts up to $50,000, higher than typical payday loans
Same-day funding available for approved applicants
Borrowers keep their vehicle while using it as collateral
No prepayment penalties according to website
Operates as licensed and regulated lender across 12+ states
Accepts alternative collateral including motorcycles and semi-trucks
Quick approval decisions claimed within minutes
cons: High-cost secured borrowing with APR rates not transparently disclosed on website
Risk of vehicle repossession if loan payments are missed
Loan terms and availability vary significantly by state, limiting consistency
Title loan dependency risk—vehicle loss could impact transportation and employment
Limited to 11 customer reviews on website, relatively small sample size for credibility
best_for: Borrowers with vehicle equity who need fast cash and have poor credit or no credit access
People facing genuine emergencies (medical bills, urgent repairs, eviction) who own a vehicle outright or nearly outright
Individuals who need larger amounts than payday loans offer but cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
diagnosis: 5 Star Car Title Loans is best for borrowers facing genuine financial emergencies who own a vehicle with equity, poor credit that disqualifies them from traditional loans, and can afford repayment to avoid repossession. The primary caveat is that title loans are high-cost secured debt with substantial repossession risk, and should only be used when no lower-cost alternatives (credit unions, installment lenders, personal loans) are available—the website does not transparently disclose APR rates, requiring borrowers to obtain quotes to understand true costs.
services: Installment loans with terms up to 24 months
In-person loan application at physical locations
Fast approval process (decision within minutes claimed)
